title: Masked data tools
text: can install development version md.tools GitHub : lot space md.tools devoted working matrices encoded columns data frames. directly store matrices column, even general lists, prefer work columns defined primitive types like boolean. , suppose boolean matrix C size 10--3: may represent data frame 10 rows columns c.1, c.2, c.3 : prefer work plaintext data files. store data frames, prefer work plaintext files like CSV files, row corresponds set measurements experimental unit. However, may also want store metadata experiment generated data, may wish store information experimental units naturally fit data frame model. store metadata, take general approach storing JSON (Javscript Object Notation) comments tabular data file (like CSV), comment default anything # character line.
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("queelius/md.tools") library(tidyverse) library(md.tools) C <- matrix(sample(c(T,F), size=3*10, replace=TRUE), nrow=10) df <- md.tools::encode_matrix(C,"c") print(df) #> # A tibble: 10 × 1 #>    c1[,1] [,2]  [,3]  #>    <lgl>  <lgl> <lgl> #>  1 TRUE   TRUE  TRUE  #>  2 FALSE  FALSE FALSE #>  3 FALSE  FALSE TRUE  #>  4 TRUE   TRUE  FALSE #>  5 FALSE  FALSE TRUE  #>  6 FALSE  TRUE  FALSE #>  7 TRUE   TRUE  TRUE  #>  8 FALSE  TRUE  TRUE  #>  9 TRUE   TRUE  FALSE #> 10 TRUE   FALSE FALSE md <- md.tools::md_read_csv_with_meta("./raw-data/exp_series_data_1.csv") #> Rows: 1000 Columns: 9 #> ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Delimiter: "," #> dbl (6): s, k, w, t.1, t.2, t.3 #> lgl (3): c.1, c.2, c.3 #>  #> ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. #> ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message. print(md) #> # A tibble: 1,000 × 9 #>          s     k     w     t.1     t.2    t.3 c.1   c.2   c.3   #>  *   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>  <dbl> <lgl> <lgl> <lgl> #>  1 0.144       2     2 0.281   0.144   0.266  TRUE  TRUE  FALSE #>  2 0.0105      1     2 0.0105  0.0141  0.0633 TRUE  FALSE TRUE  #>  3 0.0363      2     2 0.105   0.0363  0.545  TRUE  TRUE  FALSE #>  4 0.00972     1     2 0.00972 0.251   0.0960 TRUE  FALSE TRUE  #>  5 0.0377      3     2 0.0937  0.0943  0.0377 TRUE  FALSE TRUE  #>  6 0.0958      3     2 0.283   0.391   0.0958 FALSE TRUE  TRUE  #>  7 0.169       3     2 0.197   1.01    0.169  FALSE TRUE  TRUE  #>  8 0.270       3     2 0.322   0.371   0.270  FALSE TRUE  TRUE  #>  9 0.299       3     2 0.390   0.401   0.299  TRUE  FALSE TRUE  #> 10 0.00794     2     2 0.524   0.00794 0.120  FALSE TRUE  TRUE  #> # … with 990 more rows

what: Masked data for a series system with exponentially distributed nodes. — exp_series_data_1
title: Masked data for a series system with exponentially distributed nodes. — exp_series_data_1
text: Masked data containing system lifetime attributes n=1000 series system parameter value theta=c(3,4,5) candidate model m0 (md_cand_m0). candidate set specified alpha=1 size w=2.

title: Masked data for a series system with exponentially distributed nodes. — exp_series_data_1
Masked data for a series system with exponentially distributed nodes. — exp_series_data_1
text: data frame 1000 rows 9 variables: s Real observable variable, system lifetime k Integer latent variable, failed node w Integer observable variable, number candidates t.1 Real latent variable, lifetime component 1 t.2 Real latent variable, lifetime component 2 t.3 Real latent variable, lifetime component 3 c.1 Boolean observable variable, TRUE indicates component 1 candidate set c.2 Boolean observable variable, TRUE indicates component 2 candidate set c.3 Boolean observable variable, TRUE indicates component 3 candidate set

title: Masked data for a series system with exponentially distributed nodes. — exp_series_data_1
